findings.finding: Test suite cannot detect ordering violation: multi_epic.json fixture uses IDs 100/101 (same digit width); no width-mixed fixture or test specified, so lexicographic vs integer ordering coincide and pass regardless of correctness.
findings.evidence_quote: test_multi_epic_subprocess — invoke bin/cortex-build-epic-map tests/fixtures/build_epic_map/multi_epic.json; assert exit 0, parse stdout JSON, assert epic keys are '100','101' (sorted)

findings.finding: Task 1's fixture spec instructs production of 'a JSON object with version + items list' — neither is emitted by generate_json(), which dumps a bare list. Fixtures built to this description will be uniformly mis-shaped, masking the parser bug until Task 7's live invocation.
findings.reviewer_angle: Envelope shape and active-items key are unverified
findings.evidence_quote: Each fixture is a JSON object with the same top-level shape backlog/generate_index.py emits (i.e., a version field plus a list of items with id, title, status, priority, type, parent, spec, schema_version etc.).

findings.finding: Spec's runnable git diff -U0 verifications for Requirements 9f and 9g are dropped from Task 5's verification field, leaving the line-region invariant as prose-only guidance with no machine-checkable test.
findings.reviewer_angle: Spec Requirement 9f/9g (Step 3a/3c untouched) has no verification step
findings.evidence_quote: (f) Step 3a is unchanged. Verifiable: git diff -U0 skills/dev/SKILL.md shows no edits to lines 135-141 (Step 3a region). (g) Step 3c is unchanged. Verifiable: git diff -U0 skills/dev/SKILL.md shows no edits to lines 168 onward.
